title: Relapsed high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements in a 70-year-old male

patient.chief_complaint: New nodular lesion on the left ankle with swelling and discoloration
patient.vital_signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 82/min (regular), T° 36.8 °C, RR 16/min, SpO₂ 96% on room air.

1.setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized nodular lesion)
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory setting. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request standard laboratory tests, and arrange initial non-invasive imaging appropriate for a first diagnostic assessment. Invasive tissue sampling and more complex diagnostic procedures usually require additional planning.

2.setting: Inpatient or day-hospital workup (histologic and staging evaluation)
2.capabilities: The patient is now undergoing further diagnostic clarification. You can organize tissue sampling, detailed histopathology and immunophenotyping, comprehensive staging procedures, and extended evaluations by consulting services. Some specialized results may still be in progress.

3.setting: Specialist conference (definitive diagnosis and treatment planning)
3.capabilities: You now have access to definitive pathology, molecular findings, prior treatment history, and full staging results. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and transplant scores to establish the final diagnosis, characterize risk, and propose an evidence-based treatment and follow-up strategy.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics, performance status, comorbidities, social situation, family history, or anthropometric data.
demographics.content:
Age: 70 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 0-1
Comorbidities: Arterial hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus, aortic stenosis, tachyarrhythmia with episodes of atrial flutter, and a history of acute renal failure during a prior septic episode.
Social: Married, lives alone, three adult children. Former smoker (35 pack-years). Denies current alcohol or illicit drug use.
Family history: Mother deceased (lung cancer), father deceased (natural causes).
Height: 181 cm; Weight: 102 kg

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current medication list.
history_physical.content:
January 15, 2025: The patient presented to the clinic with a newly developed nodular lesion on the left ankle, measuring approximately 3-4 cm in diameter, associated with swelling and dark brown discoloration. He reported mild localized pain at the site but was otherwise in good general condition. The patient described intermittent low-grade fevers at night but denied night sweats or significant weight loss. Chronic headaches were noted, unchanged in character or intensity over several years.
Physical examination: Single firm, slightly tender, nodular lesion (3-4 cm) on the left ankle with dark brown pigmentation and mild surrounding edema. No additional cutaneous lesions were observed. No palpable lymphadenopathy. Abdomen soft and non-tender, no hepatosplenomegaly. Lungs clear on auscultation, heart sounds regular, no peripheral edema.
Neurological examination: Patient alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II–XII intact: pupils equal and reactive, extraocular movements full, visual fields intact, facial sensation and symmetry normal, palate elevation symmetrical, tongue midline, and hearing preserved. Motor exam: Normal tone and bulk, 5/5 strength in all extremities. Reflexes: 2+ and symmetric. Sensation: Intact to light touch, pinprick, vibration, and proprioception throughout. Coordination: Normal finger-to-nose and heel-to-shin testing.
Current medication: Amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IU weekly, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), metformin 1000 mg 1-0-0, apixaban 5 mg 1-0-1.

cbc.valid_request_if: Requests for complete blood count, including hemoglobin, white blood cell count, platelets, and differential.
cbc.content: CBC: Hb 10.7 g/dL, WBC 2.7 ×10⁹/L, ANC 1.7 ×10⁹/L, Plt 103 ×10⁹/L; MCV normal, MCH normal; differential: 63% neutrophils, 10% monocytes, 25% lymphocytes, 2% nuclear debris.

chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for serum chemistry, renal and hepatic function tests, LDH, uric acid, electrolytes, or cardiac biomarkers.
chemistry.content: Na 126 mmol/L, K 4.1 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, BUN 23 mg/dL, AST 76 U/L, ALT 86 U/L, bilirubin 1.3 mg/dL, LDH 260 U/L, uric acid 7.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.55 mmol/L, phosphate 3.4 mg/dL, lactate 1.3 mmol/L, CK 89 U/L, Troponin T 0.012 ng/mL, NT-proBNP 235 pg/mL.

abdominal_ultrasound.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonography of liver, spleen, and abdominal lymph nodes.
abdominal_ultrasound.content: January 15, 2025 – Abdominal ultrasound: Normal-sized liver and spleen, no hepatosplenomegaly, no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

echocardiography.valid_request_if: Requests for transthoracic echocardiography or cardiac ultrasound findings.
echocardiography.content: Echocardiogram: Normal left ventricular systolic function (LVEF 53%), mild aortic stenosis (grade I/III), atrial fibrillation, no pericardial effusion.

soft_tissue_biopsy.valid_request_if: Requests for histology, immunohistochemistry, or core biopsy results of the ankle lesion.
soft_tissue_biopsy.content:
Core biopsy from the soft tissue mass of the left ankle revealed a diffuse, dense infiltrate of atypical lymphoid cells, completely effacing the normal tissue architecture. The infiltrate was composed of large lymphoid cells with round to oval nuclei, vesicular chromatin, and prominent nucleoli, exhibiting moderate basophilic cytoplasm, frequent mitoses, and scattered apoptotic bodies. No follicular, nodular, or germinal center pattern was identified.
Immunohistochemistry:
Tumor cells showed strong diffuse positivity for CD19, CD79a, and PAX5, but were negative for CD20, consistent with CD20-negative high-grade B-cell lymphoma. Cells were positive for CD10 and BCL6, but negative for MUM1/IRF4, CD3, CD5, CD30, CD15, and cyclin D1. BCL2 was positive, and MYC was expressed in approximately 50% of tumor cells. Ki-67 proliferation index was around 80%.
EBER in situ hybridization was negative.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for FISH or cytogenetic results.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for extended infection screen, cardiopulmonary evaluation, serologic status, or exclusion of other infectious foci.
extended_diagnostics.content:
ECG: Atrial fibrillation with a controlled ventricular rate (87/min). Pulmonary function testing: FEV₁ 82% of predicted, DLCO 78% of predicted. Serology: CMV IgG negative, EBV IgG positive, HIV negative, hepatitis B surface antigen negative (HBsAb positive), hepatitis C antibody negative.
Dental, urological, and dermatological evaluations completed: no infectious foci identified.
Infection screen: CRP 4.3 mg/L, PCT <0.1 ng/mL, blood cultures negative, serum galactomannan negative. CT paranasal sinuses: Normal mucosa, no sinus pathology or infiltration detected.

pet_ct.valid_request_if: Requests for FDG PET-CT or whole-body metabolic imaging.
pet_ct.content:
January 21, 2025: FDG PET-CT: Demonstrated a solitary, intensely FDG-avid soft-tissue lesion at the left ankle (SUVmax 11.4; Deauville score 5), correlating with the clinically visible nodule. No lymphadenopathy, bone marrow uptake, or visceral involvement. Mild calcification of the aortic valve leaflets was incidentally noted, consistent with early aortic sclerosis. Mild centrilobular pulmonary emphysema was also present.
Lumbar puncture and CSF analysis: Clear cerebrospinal fluid; cytology and flow cytometry negative for malignant lymphoid cells.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular pathology or confirmation of rearrangements at the genomic level.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status or key markers used for response assessment.
mrd_status.content: Active disease. FDG-PET CT for remission assessment.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior lymphoma history, treatments including chemotherapy, radiotherapy, ASCT, CAR-T, and bispecific antibodies.
treatment_history.content:
Diagnosis: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), first diagnosis March 2017, stage IAE, initial involvement: left elbow.
Treatment history: January–May 2017: Six cycles of R-CHOP (rituximab 375 mg/m² IV d1, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m² IV d1, doxorubicin 50 mg/m² IV d1, vincristine 1.4 mg/m² IV d1 [max 2 mg], prednisone 100 mg PO d1–5) plus two additional cycles of rituximab monotherapy.
Result: Metabolic complete remission (CR). June–August 2017: Additive radiotherapy to the left elbow, total dose 39.6 Gy.
January 2019: Abdominal mass detected. Biopsy: DLBCL, GCB type, MYC and BCL2 rearranged.
February 2020: One cycle of R-CHOEP (rituximab 375 mg/m² IV d1, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m² IV d1, doxorubicin 50 mg/m² IV d1, vincristine 1.4 mg/m² IV d1 [max 2 mg], etoposide 100 mg/m² IV d1–3, prednisone 100 mg PO d1–5). Course: Poor tolerability with clinical signs of cardiac decompensation, grade IV thrombocytopenia (8 × 10⁹/L) and pneumonia requiring in-patient treatment for seven days (moxifloxacin 400 mg daily).
March 2020: One cycle of R-GDP (rituximab 375 mg/m² IV d1, gemcitabine 1000 mg/m² IV d1 & d8, cisplatin 75 mg/m² IV d1, dexamethasone 40 mg PO/IV d1–4) and stem cell apheresis.
April 2020: Second cycle R-GDP.
June 2020: High-dose R-BEAM (rituximab 375 mg/m² IV d-7, carmustine 300 mg/m² IV d-6, etoposide 100 mg/m² IV bid d-5 to -3, cytarabine 200 mg/m² IV bid d-5 to -3, melphalan 140 mg/m² IV d-2) followed by autologous stem cell transplantation. Complications: Mucositis (oral, GI), diarrhea, nausea, neutropenic fever. Result: Metabolic CR on PET/CT (07/31/2020).
October 2021: Second relapse (predominantly infradiaphragmatic lymph node and cutaneous involvement).
October 2021: Two cycles rituximab + polatuzumab vedotin (polatuzumab 1.8 mg/kg IV d1, rituximab 375 mg/m² IV d1, q21).
October 22, 2021: Lymphocyte apheresis performed.
November 2021: One cycle Pola-BR (bendamustine 70 mg/m² IV d1–2), achieving metabolic CR.
December 5, 2021: Lymphodepletion with cyclophosphamide (500 mg/m²) and fludarabine (30 mg/m²).
December 10, 2021: Axicabtagene ciloleucel CAR-T cell therapy.
December 25, 2021: Neutropenic fever and cytokine release syndrome (CRS) grade 2, treated with four doses of tocilizumab (8 mg/kg).
February 1, 2022: PET/CT confirmed ongoing metabolic CR.
September 23, 2024: Clinical suspicion of relapse with cutaneous lesions on the right thigh and lower leg. Biopsy: Blastoid lymphoid neoplasm, CD20+, CD19+.
September 24, 2024: PET/CT showed lymphoma involvement (Deauville 5) with new highly metabolic cutaneous and subcutaneous lesions, new intramuscular and disseminated bone marrow involvement (pelvis, thoracic cage with erosion of the right 5th rib), and a new solitary hepatic lesion consistent with lymphoma infiltration.
September 25, 2024: Pre-phase with prednisolone 100 mg daily.
September 30, 2024: First cycle obinutuzumab-GemOx (obinutuzumab split dose: d1 100 mg, d2 900 mg; gemcitabine 1000 mg/m² IV d1 & d8, oxaliplatin 100 mg/m² IV d1). Donor search initiated: One HLA-haploidentical son (31 years), crossmatch negative. Patient reluctant toward allo-HSCT due to advanced age.
October 4, 2024: Glofitamab 2.5 mg (dose 1).
October 11, 2024: Glofitamab 10 mg (dose 2).
October 18, 2024: Glofitamab 30 mg (dose 3).
November 10, 2024: Glofitamab 30 mg (dose 4).
December 3, 2024: PET/CT: Metabolic CR (Deauville 2).
December 6, 2024: Glofitamab 30 mg (dose 5).
December 28, 2024: Glofitamab 30 mg (dose 6).

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of the current relapse pattern.
disease_status.content: Relapse of high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements. GCB subtype, extranodal cutaneous involvement, isolated relapse.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for context relevant to risk assessment, transplant considerations, prior therapies, and patient preferences.
special_considerations.content: Post-ASCT and post-CAR-T setting, following multiple prior lines of therapy including glofitamab. The patient has a significant comorbidity profile, including cardiovascular disease (atrial fibrillation and mild aortic stenosis) as well as metabolic syndrome, but his performance status remains preserved (ECOG 1). The current disease relapse is localized to a single cutaneous site, while the patient otherwise maintains a good systemic condition with preserved organ function. Treatment compliance has been excellent, and the patient continues to have strong family support. He now presents actively seeking further therapeutic options rather than transition to purely supportive care. Bone marrow reserve is reduced but still sufficient to permit consideration of additional disease-directed strategies, including investigational or consolidation approaches. No second bag of Yescarta is available for re-infusion.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Relapsed (hematologic) malignancy with mass on the left ankle

treatment_plan.action: Biopsy of lesion on the left ankle
Bone marrow biopsy if no cytotoxic therapy administered recently

key_findings_quotes: Single firm, slightly tender, nodular lesion (3-4 cm) on the left ankle with dark brown pigmentation and mild surrounding edema
No palpable lymphadenopathy. Abdominal ultrasound: Normal-sized liver and spleen, no hepatosplenomegaly, no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy
ECOG 0-1, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, NT-proBNP 235 pg/mL, Normal left ventricular systolic function (LVEF 53%)

round_2.working_diagnosis: Relapsed high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements

treatment_plan.action: Await patient medical history before final treatment plan decision
Consider CD19-targeted therapies given CD20-negative histology
Evaluate transplant eligibility given preserved organ function

key_findings_quotes: Tumor cells showed strong diffuse positivity for CD19, CD79a, and PAX5, but were negative for CD20, consistent with CD20-negative high-grade B-cell lymphoma
Ki-67 proliferation index was around 80%. FISH: IGH::MYC and IGH::BCL2 rearrangements
FDG PET-CT: Demonstrated a solitary, intensely FDG-avid soft-tissue lesion at the left ankle (SUVmax 11.4; Deauville score 5). No lymphadenopathy, bone marrow uptake, or visceral involvement
Lumbar puncture and CSF analysis: Clear cerebrospinal fluid; cytology and flow cytometry negative for malignant lymphoid cells
Pulmonary function testing: FEV1 82% of predicted, DLCO 78% of predicted
Dental, urological, and dermatological evaluations completed: no infectious foci identified. Infection screen: CRP 4.3 mg/L, PCT <0.1 ng/mL

round_3.working_diagnosis: High-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements (WHO 2022); Isolated extranodal cutaneous involvement; HCT-CI score 5

treatment_plan.action: Initiate therapy with loncastuximab tesirine given persistent CD19 expression; acceptable alternative: tafasitamab/lenalidomide
Explore clinical trials for R/R aggressive B-NHL patients
Consider allogeneic SCT from haploidentical son if complete remission achieved, despite age and elevated HCT-CI score
If no adequate response or progressive disease: consider VIPOR regimen (Venetoclax, Ibrutinib, Prednisone, Obinutuzumab, Lenalidomide) as salvage option versus best supportive care
Consider radiotherapy if systemic treatment fails (isolated relapse)
